name: Claude Fable 5
summary: The top coding model is back worldwide after a three-week outage. It leads SWE-Bench Pro at 80.3% and runs in Claude Code, Cursor, and VS Code — use it now on Pro and Max plans to ship agentic coding work.

name: Claude Sonnet 5
summary: The default model on free and paid Claude plans now plans, uses browsers and terminals, and runs agents autonomously at near-Opus quality. Intro API pricing is $2 per million input tokens through Aug 31, making long agent runs far cheaper to ship.

name: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite Image
summary: Generate and edit images through one fast, cheap multimodal API — roughly 4 to 7 cents per image by resolution. Now generally available (Nano Banana Lite) and tuned for ultra-low latency, so it fits real-time and high-volume solo projects.

name: Gemini Omni Flash
summary: Make 3 to 10 second 720p clips from a text prompt or animate a still image, then refine them by chatting with the model. In public preview now through the Gemini API — quick, affordable video for prototypes and social posts.

name: LongCat-2.0
summary: Open-weight agentic coding model that goes toe-to-toe with GPT-5.5 on SWE-bench Pro. Grab the MIT-licensed weights on Hugging Face to self-host, or call it cheaply on OpenRouter (it topped the charts there as 'Owl Alpha'). 1M-token context fits whole repos.

name: Ornith-1.0
summary: Open-weight coding models built for AI agents, not humans — free under MIT for any use. Grab four sizes (9B up to a 397B MoE) on Hugging Face. Run the small ones locally or self-host to power your own coding agent.

name: Krea 2 (open weights)
summary: Open-weight foundation image model that makes aesthetic, style-controlled images in about 2 seconds. Grab the Raw and Turbo weights on Hugging Face (free commercial use for individuals and small teams) or call it via fal at $0.03 an image.

name: Mistral OCR 4
summary: Turn PDFs and scans into structured data — bounding boxes, typed blocks for tables, equations, and signatures, plus per-word confidence across 170 languages. Hit it via API at $4 per 1,000 pages, or self-host the container to keep documents private.
